reasoning_model_1: The model successfully identifies the core text 'How can this report have so many errors?' (00:00). However, it fails significantly on Content Accuracy by hallucinating two additional sentences: 'Are you serious? This is unacceptable' (00:01-00:05). While the added text semantically reinforces the 'Angry' emotion, the user strictly requested to read 'this sentence'. The inclusion of unprompted content violates the script constraints. Consequently, the evaluation of the 'Emotion' dimension is compromised because the model relied on adding text to express the emotion rather than modulating the prosody of the target sentence alone.
reasoning_model_2: The model strictly adheres to the provided text script, pronouncing 'How can this report have so many errors?' (00:00-00:03) without any additions or omissions, fulfilling the Content Accuracy criterion. By maintaining the script integrity, the model demonstrates the ability to apply the 'Angry' emotion through paralinguistic features (such as tone, stress, and pace) directly to the target text. The duration of approximately 3 seconds suggests a deliberate and emphatic delivery, consistent with an angry or frustrated tone.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 commits a major error in Content Accuracy by adding unrequested sentences ('Are you serious? This is unacceptable') to the audio. While this addition aligns with the emotional target, it violates the constraint to read the specific sentence provided. Model 2 correctly handles both the content constraints and the paralinguistic requirement, demonstrating true feature control on the target text. Model 1's failure to adhere to the script creates a significant gap in quality compared to the compliant generation of Model 2.
